---
title: "Class-4"
author: "Dhruvi Sheth"
date: "12 Jan 2026"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_notebook: default
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
  word_document: default
---

```{r}
x <- seq(-4, 4, length = 1000)
y <- dnorm(x, mean = 0, sd = 1)

plot(x, y, type = "l",
     main = "Normal Distribution",
     ylab = "Density", xlab = "x")

```

```{r}
x <- 0:20
y <- dbinom(x, size = 20, prob = 0.5)

barplot(y, names.arg = x,
        main = "Binomial Distribution",
        xlab = "Number of successes", ylab = "Probability")

```

```{r}
x <- 0:20
y <- dpois(x, lambda = 5)

barplot(y, names.arg = x,
        main = "Poisson Distribution",
        xlab = "Count", ylab = "Probability")

```
```{r}
set.seed(1)

normal_data  <- rnorm(10000, mean = 0, sd = 1)
binom_data   <- rbinom(10000, size = 20, prob = 0.5)
pois_data    <- rpois(10000, lambda = 5)
```

```{r}
summary(normal_data)
sd(normal_data)

summary(binom_data)
sd(binom_data)

summary(pois_data)
sd(pois_data)

```
```{r}
hist(normal_data,
     probability = TRUE,
     col = "lightblue",
     main = "Normal Distribution (mean = 0, sd = 1)",
     xlab = "Value")

# Overlay theoretical curve
x <- seq(min(normal_data), max(normal_data), length = 100)
lines(x, dnorm(x, mean = 0, sd = 1), col = "red", lwd = 2)

```
```{r}
hist(binom_data,
     probability = TRUE,
     col = "lightgreen",
     main = "Binomial Distribution (n = 20, p = 0.5)",
     xlab = "Number of Successes",
     breaks = seq(-0.5, 20.5, by = 1))

```

```{r}
hist(pois_data,
     probability = TRUE,
     col = "lightpink",
     main = "Poisson Distribution",
     xlab = "Event Count",
     breaks = seq(-0.5, max(pois_data) + 0.5, by = 1))

```


```{r}
rm(list = ls())
# nesting ls inside rm

set.seed(123)  # reproducibility
```

## 1. What is variability?

# Simulated heights of 100 individuals (cm)
```{r}

heights <- rnorm (n = 100, mean = 165, sd = 8)

hist(heights, 
     col = "lightblue",
     main = "Distribution of heights",
     xlab = "Height (cm)")

```
```{r}
mean(heights)
sd(heights)
```


```{r}
heights <- rbinom(n = 100, size = 200, prob = 165/200)

hist(heights,
     col = "lightblue",
     main = "Binomially Simulated Heights",
     xlab = "Height (cm)")
```
```{r}
mean(heights)
sd(heights)
```

## 2. Normal Distribution

```{r}
 x <- seq(130, 200, by = 0.1)
y <- dnorm(x, mean = 165, sd = 8)

plot(x, y,
     type = "l",
     lwd = 2,
     col = "blue",
     main = "Normal Distribution of Heights",
     xlab = "Height (cm)",
     ylab = "Density")

```
